---
applyTo: "**/*.{html,css,scss,js}"
description: "Use when editing frontend files for Hablemos Claro visual identity: black base palette, Spain flag accents, responsive behavior, and reuse of existing template components."
---
# Hablemos Claro Frontend Style Rules

## Brand Direction
- Base visual language: black and dark neutrals as foundation.
- Accent palette inspired by Spain flag:
  - Primary accent (dominant): yellow `#FFC400`
  - Secondary accent: red `#C8102E`
- Keep high contrast and accessible readability in all sections.

## Structural Rules
- Preserve existing page architecture and navigation patterns.
- Reuse existing template components before creating new structures.
- Keep edits targeted; avoid broad rewrites when a section-level update is enough.

## UX Rules
- Home must support: hero + manifesto + volunteer CTA + event proposals + main members + donation invitation.
- Include clear CTA hierarchy (primary in yellow, secondary in red or neutral outline).
- Use meaningful section spacing to improve scanability.

## Responsive Rules
- Mobile-first behavior is mandatory.
- Ensure all key sections stack cleanly below 992px and 768px breakpoints.
- Buttons must remain tappable and readable on small screens.

## Content Tone
- Spanish-only copy unless user explicitly requests multilingual support.
- Institutional and serious tone for manifesto and association messaging.
- Keep calls to action direct and concise.

## Donation and Social Defaults
- Donation baseline: Stripe-ready layout and content blocks.
- Social links can use placeholders until official links are provided.
